verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass TM protein (large lumenal GOLD domain, short cytoplasmic tail) that cycles between ER/ERGIC/Golgi and the plasma membrane. Its dominant compartment is the early secretory pathway, but multiple sources explicitly document PM localization — the lumenal domain overrides cytoplasmic ER-retention signals to mediate a minority surface pool. At the PM, the extracellular GOLD domain faces outward; a peptide from this domain antagonizes TGF-β signaling; it interacts with PAR2/F2RL1 at the cell surface. This is a classic dual-localization scenario: own TM anchor, documented PM pool alongside dominant intracellular compartment.
